back: A list of numbers (a vector) representing the meaning of text. Similar meanings get nearby vectors - coordinates on a map of meaning.
The list of numbers itself, e.g. [0.14, -0.88, ...]. Meaningless to humans alone; the distances between vectors carry the meaning.
Search by meaning instead of exact words: embed the query, find the nearest stored vectors.
A database built to store millions of embeddings and answer 'which points are closest to this one?' extremely fast.
Retrieve relevant passages, paste them into the prompt, and let the LLM answer from them. An open-book exam instead of memorization.

front: Why does RAG beat training on your documents?
back: No retraining cost, updates instantly when documents change, and answers can cite their sources.
Retrieval quality. If the wrong passages are found, the model builds its answer on the wrong foundation.

front: Name three products built on RAG
back: Customer-support chatbots, documentation assistants, and internal company knowledge bots (plus search engines that answer in sentences).
